meta.description: Process task backlog — concurrent with product sprints, evaluated every retrospective. See AI-Solo-Dev-Workflow-Plan Section 6.4 for the model.

scopes.global: Lives in ~/.claude/ — applies to all projects. Set up once.
scopes.project: Lives in project repo — project-specific content, paths, commands.

tasks.description: Global practice CLAUDE.md and rules — ~/.claude/CLAUDE.md with authority hierarchy rule, Producer-Verifier principle, 'explain it or reject it' cognitive debt rule, evaluation flywheel reference. ~/.claude/rules/ with modular rule files: anti-patterns.md (pattern drift + tech debt), testing-philosophy.md (verification debt), architecture.md (cognitive debt + pattern drift), security.md (standalone). These encode the practice itself, not project-specific standards.
tasks.notes: Do this first — every project inherits these. No build/test/lint commands here (those are project-specific). No @import project-context.md here (that's project-specific).

tasks.description: Project CLAUDE.md — root CLAUDE.md with build/test/lint command references, @import project-context.md (via BMAD generate-project-context), project-specific conventions. Any project-specific .claude/rules/ that don't apply universally.
tasks.notes: Deferred until a product codebase exists. Will be generated by the Momentum project bootstrap workflow (PT-021) rather than manual setup. PT-001a provides advisory coverage in the meantime.

tasks.description: Hook Infrastructure (basic) — Stop hook (test/lint gate on session end), PostToolUse auto-lint/format, PreToolUse acceptance test directory protection, PreToolUse file protection. Hook scripts reference project-specific paths and test commands.
tasks.notes: Hooks are project-scoped because they reference project-specific test commands and directory paths. Deferred until a product codebase exists. Will be generated by the Momentum project bootstrap workflow (PT-021) rather than manual setup.

tasks.description: code-reviewer sub-agent — read-only adversarial verifier at ~/.claude/agents/code-reviewer.md. Accepts story file, verifies ACs against implementation, runs tests, produces structured findings report. Pure verifier, no code modification.
tasks.notes: An early version already exists at module/canonical/agents/code-reviewer.md. This task moves it to global scope (~/.claude/agents/) and refines it based on the plan's requirements. Available to all projects once done.

tasks.description: Process Task Backlog add/resolve workflows — two simple skills or lightweight workflows. Add: accepts description, priority, scope, source, agents, appends to backlog JSON and regenerates markdown view. Resolve: marks done, records resolution, regenerates markdown.
tasks.notes: The workflows themselves are reusable across projects (global skills). They operate on whichever project's backlog file is in the current working directory. The backlog file itself (this file) is project-scoped.

tasks.description: TEA Integration (ATDD gate for Dev Story) — modify Dev Story workflow so it checks for ATDD test files before implementation. Halt if no ATDD tests exist. Also: TEA Automate after implementation for coverage expansion.
tasks.notes: BMAD workflow customization via wrapper (Section 8.1 Layer C). Candidate for Momentum module if reusable across projects.

tasks.description: BMAD Code Review: enforce pure verifier role — remove option 1 ('fix automatically') from code review workflow. Reviewer outputs findings only; remediation happens in subsequent Dev Story session.
tasks.notes: BMAD workflow customization. Candidate for Momentum module.

tasks.description: Upstream Fix Discipline — create /upstream-fix skill that analyzes quality failures and proposes upstream corrections. Begin maintaining lightweight decision log.
tasks.notes: Practice-level skill, reusable across projects.

tasks.description: Findings ledger — create docs/quality/findings-ledger.json. Fields: story ID, finding category, description, root cause classification, upstream level, resolution. Retrospective reads this for cross-story pattern detection.
tasks.notes: Project-specific data. The schema is reusable but the data is per-project.

tasks.description: Quality Rules File — project-specific standards loaded by BMAD review workflows alongside generic analysis.
Stop Hook Quality Gate (Phase 2 enhancement) — add conditional logic to basic Stop hook: check git diff for code vs docs-only changes, skip tests for docs sessions, optionally check agent_type.
tasks.notes: Enhancement to PT-002. Depends on PT-002 being done first.

tasks.description: architecture-guard sub-agent — read-only, persistent memory at ~/.claude/agents/architecture-guard.md. Detects pattern drift across sessions by comparing new code against CLAUDE.md and architecture docs.
tasks.notes: Becomes valuable after several sprints of accumulated code. Not useful on sprint 1.

tasks.description: CLAUDE.md Generation — skill or workflow that auto-generates .claude/rules/ content from BMAD Architecture docs and PRD constraints. Ensure CLAUDE.md @imports project-context.md.
tasks.notes: Automates what can be done manually. Nice-to-have.

tasks.description: Automated Flywheel — wrap Retrospective workflow (via custom module wrapper, Layer C) to integrate findings ledger, upstream trace categorization, and produce CLAUDE.md amendments, quality rule updates, DoD additions. Create /apply-retro-learnings skill.
tasks.notes: Can run retrospectives manually and apply learnings by hand until this exists. Candidate for Momentum module.

tasks.description: Discord MCP Integration — read-only Discord bot + MCP server for BMAD Discord monitoring. Requires admin approval (requested 2026-03-08).
tasks.notes: Externally gated on BMAD server admin approval. Manual fallback works. MCP config is global (all projects benefit).

tasks.description: Pipeline Orchestrator — new BMAD workflow chaining full pipeline: PRD Validate → Architecture → Implementation Readiness → Sprint Plan → (per story: ATDD → Dev → Code Review → Test Automation) → Retrospective → Apply Learnings.
tasks.notes: The 'Level 4 unlock.' Depends on all other process infrastructure working first. Candidate for Momentum module.

tasks.description: Model Routing Strategy — model selection guide as canonical module resource (condensed from benchmarking research Section 1.4 task-type mapping + Section 8 decision matrix), default model: and effort frontmatter for all momentum agents and skills, and a rule in .claude/rules/ about when to override model defaults. Produces: module/canonical/resources/model-routing-guide.md, model/effort frontmatter on all agents/skills, module/canonical/rules/model-routing.md.
tasks.notes: Elevated from Phase 3/low to high priority based on multi-model benchmarking research (2026-03-14). Scope expanded from single skill to comprehensive model routing strategy. Produces three artifacts: (1) model-routing-guide.md condensing benchmarking guide Sections 1.4 and 8, (2) model/effort frontmatter defaults on all canonical agents and skills, (3) model-routing.md rule for .claude/rules/. Includes cognitive hazard argument: for outputs without automated validation, use flagship models.

tasks.description: Property-Based Testing — TEA extension deriving properties and invariants from specifications. Generates Hypothesis/fast-check tests.
Architectural Fitness Tests — automated tests encoding structural rules (dependency direction, module boundaries, import policies).
tasks.notes: Requires enough code to have architecture worth testing.

tasks.description: Mutation Testing — tool integration (Stryker/PIT/mutmut) to verify tests are asserting meaningful behavior. Run against dev agent's implementation tests.
tasks.notes: Optional even in original Phase 3.

tasks.description: Hybrid Research Workflow — custom BMAD workflow codifying the three-step hybrid research process (Analyst prompt prep → external deep research → synthesis).
tasks.notes: Practice-level workflow via Momentum module. Useful for research but doesn't block product development.

tasks.description: Momentum module — create the cross-project custom BMAD module with three roles: (1) `momentum install` workflow that deploys canonical global practice files (rules, agents, CLAUDE.md) from Momentum to ~/.claude/ — idempotent, re-runnable, solves new-machine problem; (2) `momentum bootstrap` interactive workflow that scaffolds project-specific config (project CLAUDE.md, hooks, findings ledger, process backlog, quality rules) based on developer decisions about stack/framework/test commands — including cost observability setup: showTurnDuration in settings.json, ccusage as recommended install, and optional OTel configuration (CLAUDE_CODE_ENABLE_TELEMETRY=1) for projects that need telemetry — run once, output committed to project repo; (3) home for operational workflows (backlog add/resolve, retrospective wrapper, hybrid research, etc.) that run for the life of the project.
tasks.notes: The module is the source of truth for global practice — ~/.claude/ is deployed state, not canonical. Bootstrap generates project config that gets committed to git; second person cloning the project gets it via git clone, no bootstrap needed. Operational workflows (backlog, retro wrapper, etc.) keep running after bootstrap is done. Bootstrap also sets up cost observability: showTurnDuration: true in .claude/settings.json, recommendation to install ccusage for cross-session cost tracking, and optional CLAUDE_CODE_ENABLE_TELEMETRY=1 for OTel export. These are part of the cost management discipline added in the March 2026 plan update.

tasks.description: Benchmarking Harness — build the runnable testing infrastructure for empirical model routing decisions. Five deliverables from the benchmarking handoff: (1) promptfoo configuration for BMAD skills using Claude Agent SDK provider, (2) bash benchmarking script (claude -p --model X --output-format json for each model), (3) golden dataset starter (5-10 reference outputs per skill type), (4) Pydantic AI benchmarking harness using agent.override() + Pydantic Evals, (5) model configuration (model:/effort frontmatter) for existing skills and agents.
tasks.planReference: Benchmarking handoff deliverables 1-5, benchmarking guide Sections 4.2, 5.3, 6.4
tasks.notes: Implementation-ready based on completed research. PT-016 (Model Routing Strategy) produces the decision framework; this task produces the tooling to validate those decisions empirically. Depends on PT-016 for the model selection guide that determines what to benchmark. Key tools: promptfoo (Claude Agent SDK provider), claude -p --output-format json, Pydantic Evals.

tasks.description: Impetus: Journal script commands — create momentum-tools.py commands for deterministic journal operations currently performed by the LLM. Three commands: (1) `session journal-hygiene` returning structured JSON with sorted threads, warnings (concurrent, dormant, dependency-satisfied, unwieldy), and suppressed offers; (2) `session journal-write` for append + view regeneration; (3) extend `session journal-status --hygiene` with pre-computed results. Eliminates 800-1300 tokens/session on the open-threads path and removes fragile LLM behavior (JSONL parsing, timestamp arithmetic, context-hash computation, markdown table rendering from parsed data).
tasks.planReference: Both runs: script-opportunities workflow.md:390-512, enhancement-opportunities journal corruption/concurrency
tasks.notes: The preflight script already demonstrates the right architecture. These are the highest-token-cost deterministic operations remaining. Also addresses journal corruption resilience and concurrent session safety.

tasks.description: Impetus: Install/upgrade I/O consolidation — batch install and upgrade action execution into a single `session install-actions` momentum-tools.py command. Currently Step 3-4 performs 7+ sequential Bash calls for file copies, inline python3 one-liners for state file writes, and sequential per-action shell execution. A single script command accepting an action list would return structured results. Also batch hash-drift resolution execution (manual file copy loop) and upgrade chain resolution (linked-list traversal by LLM). Estimated savings: 800-1200 tokens on install path, 300-500 on upgrade chain.
tasks.notes: Infrequent paths (install/upgrade) but high token cost when hit. Extends the preflight pattern to cover remaining deterministic operations.

tasks.description: Impetus: Sprint health summary in greeting — add quantitative sprint context to the preflight greeting data. Currently the greeting has strong voice but zero orientation on sprint progress. The developer's most urgent question at session start ('where are things?') is deferred behind a dispatch to sprint-dev. Include a one-line sprint health summary in preflight output (e.g., '2 of 4 stories done, 1 in progress, 1 ready'). When momentum_completions is high and exactly one obvious action exists, offer an express greeting: single-line state + single-key dispatch, with full menu one keystroke away.
tasks.planReference: Run 2: enhancement-opportunities session-greeting.md, SKILL.md happy path
tasks.notes: Transforms the greeting from identity moment to orientation moment. The express mode component depends on sufficient session history (momentum_completions >= 10).

tasks.description: Impetus: Onboarding, help command, and fallback paths — three UX gaps: (1) Post-install onboarding: after first-install completes, new users face a sprint menu with no mental model of Momentum concepts. Add a fork offering a quick walkthrough or jump-to-planning. (2) Help command: no response for 'help' or '?' input. Add a brief self-description and menu explanation to the input interpretation system. (3) Fallback routing: SKILL.md startup has no fallback for unexpected preflight results. Add a catch-all that surfaces the unexpected state and offers manual recovery. Also: decline-path degraded mode is invisible — user who declines setup gets no 'what is Momentum?' off-ramp.
tasks.planReference: Both runs: agent-cohesion SKILL.md Startup, enhancement-opportunities Steps 2-7
tasks.notes: Affects first-session retention and error recovery. The fallback routing is the most critical piece — unexpected preflight results currently produce undefined behavior.

tasks.description: Impetus: Hygiene signal triage — Step 11 presents concurrent-tab warnings, dormant thread offers, dependency-satisfied notifications, and unwieldy-triage offers all at once, inline, before the selection prompt. For messy journals with 6+ threads this creates an overwhelming wall of text with no priority differentiation. Safety warnings (concurrent tabs) appear at the same visual weight as convenience suggestions (dormant cleanup). Fix: lead with safety signals, display thread list, surface one hygiene observation (most impactful) before selection prompt, gate remaining items behind 'more hygiene items?' soft prompt. Add session-level 'stop offering dormant cleanup' preference after 3 consecutive declines.
tasks.notes: Signal dilution in the most complex journal states. Developers with messy state learn to ignore hygiene outputs — the opposite of the intended effect.

tasks.description: Impetus: Dispatch target mismatch for 'Refine backlog' — cross-file inconsistency between SKILL.md (dispatches to momentum:refine), session-greeting.md reference, and workflow.md. Audit all three files and determine the canonical dispatch target, then fix the mismatches. Also fix the retro dispatch placeholder which surfaces a raw CLI command ('momentum-tools sprint retro-complete'), violating the voice rule against exposing backstage machinery.
tasks.planReference: Run 2: agent-cohesion dispatch table, voice rule violation in retro placeholder
tasks.notes: The dispatch mismatch is a bug. The retro placeholder voice violation is a quick fix. Bundled because both are dispatch-table issues.

tasks.description: Impetus: Hash-drift accept option and input edge cases — (1) Hash drift [K]eep option creates a recurring warning every session with no permanent accept. Add an [A]ccept option that records the current hash as intentional, suppressing future warnings for that file. (2) Out-of-range menu selection produces undefined behavior. Add input validation that re-prompts on invalid selection. (3) Empty journal files and momentum-tools.py failures produce silent failures with no guidance.
tasks.planReference: Run 1: enhancement-opportunities Step 10, Run 2: hostile-environment findings
tasks.notes: Defensive improvements. The hash-drift accept option was flagged by both runs. Error handling items prevent silent failures in hostile environments.

tasks.description: Impetus: Express mode for high-frequency users — developers who invoke /momentum multiple times daily experience full greeting ceremony every time. When momentum_completions crosses a threshold (suggest >= 10) and exactly one obvious action exists, offer an express greeting: single-line state + single-key dispatch, with full menu one keystroke away. Also consider a 'what changed since last session?' digest for returning users showing recent commits, story state changes, or sprint progress deltas.
tasks.notes: Medium impact for daily power users. Low priority because few users have hit the friction threshold yet (current max: 4 completions). Becomes more valuable as adoption grows.

tasks.description: Impetus: Path portability — 16 instances of ~/  home directory paths across workflow.md and eval files. Workflow.md uses paths like ~/.claude/momentum/global-installed.json and ~/.claude/rules/ in LLM instructions. Eval files embed these in Given/When/Then assertions. Evaluate whether to: (a) replace workflow.md paths with $HOME expansion or script-resolved paths, (b) exempt eval files from the path-standards rule since they describe scenarios rather than execute paths, (c) have momentum-tools.py resolve paths internally so hardcoded paths stay out of prompts.
tasks.planReference: Both runs: path-standards workflow.md:159-599, eval files
tasks.notes: Debatable severity. The LLM resolves ~/ correctly in practice. Eval files use ~/ as natural shorthand in scenario descriptions. The portability concern is theoretical (non-standard home directory configs, CI). May be better addressed by updating the path-standards scanner to exempt behavioral evals rather than changing the files.

tasks.description: Impetus: SKILL.md section structure alignment — SKILL.md uses non-standard headings (Startup, Voice & Input, Runtime Behaviors) instead of canonical BMad template sections (Overview, Identity, Communication Style, Principles, On Activation). All content exists but under different names or relocated to workflow.md. Options: (a) add thin canonical section wrappers that reference existing content, (b) rename existing sections to match template, (c) accept as intentional lean bootloader deviation and document it. The missing Overview is the most impactful gap — on the happy path (which never loads workflow.md) the agent operates without its mission framing paragraph.
tasks.planReference: Both runs: structure-prepass SKILL.md, structure-analysis identity duplication
tasks.notes: Debatable. The lean bootloader pattern is intentional — adding sections increases token cost on every invocation (~1036 tokens currently). The strongest argument for change is the missing Overview: after context compaction drops workflow.md, the agent has no mission understanding. A 3-sentence Overview would be cheap insurance.
